fantasy-cup.name: Steel Wall
fantasy-cup.why: Double Steel core walls most threats. Togekiss covers Fighting weakness with Fairy/Flying and hits Dragons hard.

fantasy-cup.name: Fairy Pressure
fantasy-cup.why: Azumarill leads with bulk and Fairy coverage. Gardevoir nukes Dragons. Steelix tanks everything else and resists Poison.

fantasy-cup.name: Dragon Control
fantasy-cup.why: Altaria pressures other Dragons. G-Stunfisk counters Steel and Fairy types. Azumarill fills coverage gaps with Water/Fairy.

great-league.name: ABB Classic
great-league.why: Medicham leads and baits Fighting counters. Two Steel closers — once the opponent burns resources on one, the other sweeps.

great-league.name: Tanky Core
great-league.why: Three bulky Pokemon that cover each other's weaknesses. Azumarill handles Fire/Ground, Bastiodon walls Flyers, Trevenant hits Water types.

great-league.name: Shadow Rush
great-league.why: Fast energy generation across the board. Sableye has no weaknesses in practice, Swampert spams Hydro Cannon, Medicham stacks Power-Up Punch.

ultra-league.name: Giratina Core
ultra-league.why: Giratina dominates neutral matchups. Registeel walls Fairies that threaten Giratina. Swampert covers Steel and Fire types.

ultra-league.name: Fighter Lead
ultra-league.why: Machamp pressures Registeel and Snorlax leads. Togekiss covers Fighting weakness and handles Dragons. Trevenant rounds out with Ghost/Grass coverage.

ultra-league.name: Dragon Slayer
ultra-league.why: Built to beat Giratina teams. Togekiss double-resists Dragon. Registeel handles Fairies. Swampert covers the Steel matchups.

master-league.name: Steel Core
master-league.why: Zacian dominates Fairies and Dragons. Metagross covers Fairy/Ice. Palkia adds Dragon+Water power with Aqua Tail spam.

master-league.name: Dragon Trinity
master-league.why: Triple dragon with diverse typing. Palkia resists Fire+Water, Dialga resists Fairy, Kyurem nukes with Fusion Flare + Ice.

master-league.name: Balance
master-league.why: Xerneas leads with Fairy dominance. Metagross handles Ice+Fairy. Kyogre sweeps with Water+Ice coverage.

spring-cup.name: Water Wall
spring-cup.why: Triple Water core with Steel/Poison/Ghost coverage. Empoleon resists Fairy, Tentacruel poisons Grass threats, Jellicent blocks Fighting.

spring-cup.name: Fairy Rush
spring-cup.why: Tinkaton leads with Fairy+Steel. Klefki adds Foul Play pressure. Araquanid patches Water coverage and resists Fire.

spring-cup.name: Grass Control
spring-cup.why: Ferrothorn walls Water and Fairy. Shadow Victreebel hits fast with Leaf Blade. Walrein closes with Ice coverage on Grass mirrors.
